name: Red Acre
description: A compact, deeply colored red cabbage with dense, slightly peppery leaves. Color deepens from purple to near-red after cold exposure. One of the best red cabbages for northern climates.
history: An American heirloom from the early 1900s, bred for reliability in short-season northern climates. Has remained a standard for home gardeners who want a compact, dependable red cabbage without the long season requirements of European types.
seasonal_quirks: Color deepens dramatically after the first frost — plan fall plantings to catch cold weather. Anthocyanin content increases in cold, making leaves more nutritious and more colorful.
harvest_cues: Heads should feel very firm — red cabbage is denser than green. 4–6 inches across, leaves tightly packed.
variety_notes: The red color bleeds into pickling liquid, creating gorgeous pink-purple pickles. Add vinegar when cooking to preserve the color — alkaline conditions turn it blue-gray.
preservation: Excellent pickled. Ferments into a beautiful pink sauerkraut. Stores well in cold conditions.
